message:
chore(beads): mark ntm-yl9x (Files Panel) as closed

The Files Panel feature (ntm-yl9x) has been implemented and is now
integrated into the TUI dashboard sidebar.

Implementation details:
- New FilesPanel in internal/tui/dashboard/panels/files.go
- Displays file modifications attributed to specific agents
- Height-gated rendering with min/max constraints
- Full test coverage added

Assigned to: BluePond
Closed: 2025-12-12

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-951","title":"Bug: Harden SSH remote tmux arg quoting","description":"Remote tmux execution uses: ssh host tmux <args...>. OpenSSH builds a single remote command string, so args with spaces/metacharacters can break and enable remote-shell injection. Fix by constructing a single remote command with strict shell-escaping (tmux + each arg), add unit tests, and ensure all remote tmux calls are safe/reliable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-12T11:34:14.088049176-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T11:40:41.389206298-06:00","closed_at":"2025-12-12T11:40:41.389206298-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-951","depends_on_id":"ntm-5zs","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952","title":"EPIC: Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)","description":"# Premium UX Polish (Stripe-level Terminal UX)

## Goal
Make NTM feel calm, obvious, and premium — like a best-in-class product — while staying true to terminal constraints.

This is a **polish program epic**: it coordinates UX improvements across CLI + TUI, emphasizing:
- Clear information hierarchy (less visual noise)
- Predictable navigation (stable keymaps + help overlays)
- Actionable copy (every error tells you what to do next)
- Smoothness (minimal flicker/jitter, fast refresh, responsive interactions)
- Accessibility (reduced motion, icon fallbacks, contrast)

## Target User Journeys
1. **First run**: install → `ntm deps` → `ntm tutorial` → `ntm spawn` → `ntm palette` → `ntm dashboard`
2. **Daily loop**: attach → palette → send → dashboard → copy/save → detach
3. **Remote over SSH**: sessions persist, minimal surprises, no brittle quoting, clear "where am I" context

## Scope
- CLI: copywriting, error remediation, progress affordances
- Palette: search + target selection clarity + recents/favorites
- Dashboard: stable layout + help/legend + better empty/loading/error states
- Theme/accessibility: reduced motion + safe icon fallbacks + contrast
- Performance: refresh budgets, caching, graceful degradation
- Docs alignment (update existing docs; avoid new doc sprawl)

## Out of Scope (tracked elsewhere)
- New major product surfaces (e.g., VS Code extension) → see `ntm-97c5`
- New data sources/panels already covered under dashboard program → see `ntm-jpp1`
- Large config discovery work → see `ntm-bke.1`

## Related / Dependencies (foundations)
- Dashboard foundation: `ntm-jpp1`, `ntm-im5l`, `ntm-p99w`, `ntm-amou`
- Width + layout primitives: `ntm-ci35`
- Refresh cadence: `ntm-0c8q`
- Theme system: `ntm-ed5`, `ntm-ube`
- Context/session inference: `ntm-4ji`, `ntm-bke.1`","design":"## Design Principles (Stripe-level in terminal)
- **Clarity over cleverness**: effects exist to support hierarchy, not compete with content.
- **Predictability**: stable keymaps; no surprise mode switches; consistent back/escape behavior.
- **Actionability**: errors include cause + fix + suggested next command.
- **Continuity**: preserve scroll position / selection where possible; avoid jitter.
- **Graceful fallback**: icons, colors, widths, and animations degrade cleanly.","acceptance_criteria":"- A consistent help/legend affordance exists in palette + dashboard.
- CLI errors are standardized and always include a remediation suggestion.
- Reduced-motion mode exists and disables pulsing/shimmer animations.
- Dashboard/palette interactions feel stable (minimal flicker/jump under refresh).
- Docs reflect the actual UX (keys, tiers, environment variables).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:30:47.75131848-06:00"}
+{"id":"ntm-952.1","title":"SUB-EPIC: UX Design System & Consistency","description":"Focus: consistency and “calm premium” feel across all TUIs.

Deliverables:
- Shared layout primitives and style tokens (spacing, borders, typography)
- Shared interaction patterns (help overlay, key hints, empty/loading states)
- A small set of conventions used everywhere (no per-screen one-offs)

Non-goals:
- Rewriting all screens at once
- Adding new major product features","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:32:05.668396626-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.1","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:

- Better session selection/resolution when args omitted
- Consistent “what next” guidance after major commands","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.767463221-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.2","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:
- Recents/favorites/pinning
- Crystal-clear target selection (who will receive this?)
- Preview that reduces misfires (metadata, warnings)
- Help overlay and consistent navigation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.793248034-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.793248034-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.3","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
+{"id":"ntm-952.4","title":"SUB-EPIC: Dashboard Premium","description":"Focus: dashboard interactions, information hierarchy, and stability under refresh.

Deliverables:
- Help/legend overlay and consistent keymap
- Better empty/loading/error states with retry affordances
- Reduced layout jitter/flicker
- Quick actions that match the real workflow","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.819145086-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.819145086-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.4","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

Deliverables:

- Safer icon and width fallbacks (no broken gutters)
- Better contrast defaults and an escape hatch for low-color terminals","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.84566752-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.5","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

- Graceful degradation when tmux data is missing/slow
- Debug affordances for diagnosing “why is this slow?”","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.871798196-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.6","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}

- Add workflow cookbook sections in existing docs (avoid new doc sprawl)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.898073661-06:00","updated_at":"2025-12-12T12:33:24.898073661-06:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-952.7","depends_on_id":"ntm-952","type":"parent-child","created_at":"0001-01-01T00:00:00Z","created_by":""}]}
 {"id":"ntm-97c5","title":"EPIC: IDE Integration (VS Code)","description":"# IDE Integration (VS Code Extension)

## Background & Motivation

Most developers work primarily in VS Code. IDE integration reduces context switching and enables unique value that terminal-only can't provide.

## CRITICAL: Focus on Unique IDE Value

The terminal TUI is already excellent. Don't rebuild it in VS Code.

**Unique IDE value:**
1. **Editor context injection**: Send selected code/current file to agents
2. **Quick access**: Status bar for at-a-glance info
3. **File decorations**: Show lock status in explorer

**NOT prioritized:**
- Full dashboard webview (duplicates TUI)
- Complex session management (use terminal)

## What the Extension Provides

### 1. Status Bar (High Value, Low Effort)
```
[NTM: myproject ● 4 agents]
```
- Click to open command palette
- Shows session name, agent count
- Color indicates status (green=ok, yellow=warning)

### 2. Commands (High Value, Medium Effort)
```
NTM: Send Selection to Agent
NTM: Send Current File to Agent
NTM: Spawn Agents for Project
NTM: Show Session Status
NTM: Open Terminal (attach to session)
```

### 3. Context Injection (Killer Feature)
```typescript
// When user invokes "Send Selection"
const selection = editor.selection;
const text = editor.document.getText(selection);
const filePath = editor.document.fileName;

// Construct context-aware prompt
const prompt = `
File: ${filePath}
Selected code:
\`\`\`
${text}
\`\`\`

[User's instruction here]
`;

// Send via ntm send
await ntm.send(session, target, prompt);
```

### 4. File Decorations (Medium Value, Medium Effort)
Show lock status in file explorer:
- 🔒 icon for locked files
- Tooltip: "Locked by GreenCastle (cc_1)"
- Requires Agent Mail integration

### 5. Simple Session View (Optional)
Activity bar panel with:
- List of sessions
- Agent count per session
- Quick actions (attach, kill)

NOT a full dashboard - just navigation.

## What We're NOT Building

- ❌ Full webview dashboard (use terminal TUI)
- ❌ Inline agent chat (complex, scope creep)
- ❌ Custom editor for prompts (overkill)

## Technical Approach

### CLI Wrapper
```typescript
class NtmClient {
    async getStatus(): Promise<RobotStatus> {
        const result = await exec('ntm --robot-status');
        return JSON.parse(result.stdout);
    }
    
    async send(session: string, target: string, prompt: string): Promise<void> {
        await exec(`ntm send ${session} ${target} ${quote(prompt)}`);
    }
}
```

### Extension Activation
Activate when:
- Workspace contains .ntm/ directory
- User runs NTM command
- NTM status bar clicked

## Priority Order

1. **P2**: Status bar + basic commands (NTM: Spawn, Send)
2. **P2**: Context injection (Send Selection)
3. **P3**: File decorations (requires Agent Mail)
4. **P3**: Session tree view
5. **P4**: Webview (if ever)

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Status bar shows current session
- [ ] Commands in palette for common actions
- [ ] "Send Selection to Agent" works with context
- [ ] Extension published to marketplace
- [ ] Minimal dependencies, fast activation
- [ ] Degrades gracefully if ntm not installed","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:28:29.775574-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:37:02.457909-05:00"}

 {"id":"ntm-yxa","title":"Implement session agent registration","description":"# Implement Session Coordinator Registration

## Purpose

Register NTM as a COORDINATOR agent with Agent Mail - not as a replacement for the actual agents, but as the orchestration layer.

## IMPORTANT CLARIFICATION

NTM registers as a **coordinator**, not as the agents themselves:
- Identity: `ntm_<session>` (e.g., `ntm_myproject`)
- Program: `ntm`
- Role: Coordinator/orchestrator
- Purpose: User-initiated messages, session-level file reservations

The actual Claude/Codex/Gemini agents may have their own Agent Mail identities (e.g., `GreenCastle`, `BlueLake`). NTM doesn't impersonate them.

## Registration Flow

```go
func RegisterSessionCoordinator(client *agentmail.Client, sessionName, projectDir string) (*agentmail.Agent, error) {
    // 1. Ensure project exists
    if _, err := client.EnsureProject(projectDir); err != nil {
        return nil, err
    }
    
    // 2. Register coordinator identity
    return client.RegisterAgent(RegisterAgentInput{
        ProjectKey:      projectDir,
        Program:         "ntm",
        Model:           "coordinator",
        Name:            fmt.Sprintf("ntm_%s", sanitize(sessionName)),
        TaskDescription: "NTM session coordinator - user-initiated actions",
    })
}
```

## When Registration Happens

- **On spawn**: Register coordinator after session creation
- **On attach**: Update last_active_ts (agent already exists)
- **Optional**: Can skip if Agent Mail unavailable

## Discovering Other Agents

After registration, NTM can discover other agents in the project:
```go
agents, _ := client.ListAgents(projectDir)
// Returns: GreenCastle (claude-code), BlueLake (claude-code), RedStone (codex-cli), etc.
```

This enables the aggregate inbox view.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Coordinator registered as `ntm_<session>` on spawn
- [ ] Registration is idempotent (attach doesn't create duplicate)
- [ ] Other project agents discoverable via ListAgents
- [ ] Registration failures don't block session creation
- [ ] Clear distinction: NTM is coordinator, not the agents","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:18:03.476453-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:01.613618-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T03:36:01.613618-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yxa","depends_on_id":"ntm-uwa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:55.757544-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-yyh","title":"Integration Test: status detection with real panes","description":"# Integration Test: Status Detection

## Scope
Verify agent status detection works with real tmux pane output.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Idle Detection
- Create pane with shell prompt
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as idle

### Scenario 2: Working Detection
- Create pane with active output
- Run status detection
- Verify detected as working

### Scenario 3: Error Detection
- Create pane with error message (rate limit, auth, etc.)
- Run status detection
- Verify error detected and classified

### Scenario 4: Agent Type Detection
- Create panes with different agent titles
- Run type detection
- Verify Claude, Codex, Gemini correctly identified

### Scenario 5: ANSI Handling
- Create pane with colored output
- Run detection
- Verify ANSI codes don't interfere

## Implementation
- tests/integration/status_test.go
- Create real tmux panes
- Send specific content via tmux send-keys
- Run detection functions
- Verify results","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:18.827555-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T10:37:20.707413-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T10:37:20.707413-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-yyh","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:41.737081-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-yyh","depends_on_id":"ntm-7lk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.80065-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
